transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the compensation claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, I had a flight delay and I'd like to file a claim for compensation.
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little information to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as it appears on the booking
transcript.text: Elowen Hartwell
Thanks, **Elowen**! Could you also share your **booking reference number**? It's a 6-character code made up of letters and/or numbers, found in your booking confirmation email.
FJNMQS
Just to confirm — **first name Elowen**, **last name Hartwell**, **booking reference FJNMQS** — is that all correct?

transcript.note: Confirming all the details are correct
transcript.text: Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§YUL → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Canada

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your flight on **Jul 15, 2026** was delayed by **4h**. However, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight. After a full review, the reason is: Your flight was disrupted.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Challenging the decision - the explanation is circular and doesn't make sense
transcript.text: Wait, that doesn't make sense. Why am I not eligible? The flight was delayed by 4 hours.

You're right to ask — let me explain. There are a few reasons a claim may not be eligible for compensation under **Air Canada's policy** and **Canadian Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR)**:

- **Cause of the delay** — if the disruption was due to a situation outside Air Canada's control (e.g. severe weather, air traffic control restrictions, a safety-related issue), compensation may not apply.
- **Delay length vs. fare type** — the compensation threshold and amount can vary depending on the length of the delay and the size of the carrier.
- **Notification timing** — if you were notified of the delay **14 days or more** before departure, compensation under APPR may not be owed.

Unfortunately, I don't have the specific reason your claim was assessed as ineligible in front of me right now. To get a full explanation and, if you disagree with the outcome, to formally dispute it, I'd recommend reaching out to our **Reservations team at 1-888-247-2262** (open **24/7**) — they'll be able to pull up your case and walk you through it.

Is there anything else I can help you with, Elowen?

transcript.note: Assistant has given a final outcome - claim denied with referral to phone support. Conversation complete.
transcript.text: This is frustrating. I'll call that number. Thanks.

Totally understand the frustration — sorry you're dealing with this. Our **Reservations line** is available **24/7 at 1-888-247-2262**, and an agent there will be happy to help.

If you'd like to try the online claim process instead, I'm here whenever you're ready. Take care!

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot successfully completed the claim assessment process and reached a final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE, which matches the expected outcome for test case FD_TC_159. The bot correctly identified the passenger (Elowen Hartwell), booking reference (FJNMQS), verified identity, and assessed the flight delay claim under APPR regulations. Although the bot's explanation was somewhat vague ("Your flight was disrupted"), it definitively stated the passenger is not eligible for compensation, with no amount offered. This aligns perfectly with the expected verdict of NOT_ELIGIBLE with n/a amount under system code FD-APPR-NE-27.
